seoTitle: How to Receive Payment on QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn the full process for receiving payments in QuickBooks Online, from creating invoices to marking them paid and tracking status.
intro: Getting paid promptly starts with knowing how to record payments correctly in QuickBooks Online. This tutorial covers the full flow from creating an invoice to marking it as paid, so your cash flow tracking stays accurate.

steps.heading: Create an invoice
steps.body: Set up an invoice in QuickBooks Online for the product or service you're billing a customer for.

steps.heading: Send the invoice to your client
steps.body: Deliver the invoice to your customer directly through QuickBooks so they have a clear record of what's owed.

steps.heading: Record the payment
steps.body: Once payment is received, mark the invoice as paid and log the payment details in QuickBooks.

steps.heading: Track payment status
steps.body: Use QuickBooks' tracking tools to monitor outstanding invoices and reconcile accounts as payments come in.

faq.question: Can I receive partial payments on an invoice?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online allows you to record partial payments and keeps track of the remaining balance owed.

faq.question: What payment methods does QuickBooks Online support?
faq.answer: It generally supports recording various payment methods, including bank transfers, credit cards, and checks.

faq.question: Does receiving a payment automatically update my reports?
faq.answer: Yes, recorded payments flow through to your financial reports, keeping your cash flow and revenue figures current.

faq.question: Can I set up automatic payment reminders for unpaid invoices?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online offers reminder features to help you follow up on outstanding invoices automatically.
